title: Adjust default config file location to match the upstream
summary: loki-rock ships its default config at /etc/loki/loki-local-config.yaml, a slight deviation from the upstream Loki Dockerfile which uses /etc/loki/local-config.yaml. The issue asks to rename the file to local-config.yaml and update the pebble/service command to point at the new path so the rock matches upstream (and the other HA rocks).
why_good_first_issue: Maintainer-filed, tightly scoped, with the exact source and target paths spelled out. The change is a file rename plus two string updates in rockcraft.yaml (the organize: mapping and the service command), no build logic. Clear done-state; easy to validate with goss (the /config endpoint). Great first PR.
affected_files: 3.7.2/rockcraft.yaml (service command + default-config organize); 3.7.2/loki-local-config.yaml (rename to local-config.yaml); optionally the same in older version dirs if applied retrospectively
repro_steps: gh issue view 81 -R canonical/loki-rock; gh api repos/canonical/loki-rock/contents/3.7.2/rockcraft.yaml --jq .content | base64 -d | grep -nE 'config.file|organize|loki-local-config'; gh api repos/canonical/loki-rock/contents/3.7.2 --jq '.[].name'; gh api repos/grafana/loki/contents/cmd/loki/Dockerfile --jq .content | base64 -d | grep -iE 'local-config|CMD'.
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: 3.7.2/rockcraft.yaml line 9 command is '/bin/loki --config.file=/etc/loki/loki-local-config.yaml' and line 41 organizes 'loki-local-config.yaml: etc/loki/loki-local-config.yaml' (file 3.7.2/loki-local-config.yaml exists). Upstream grafana/loki cmd/loki/Dockerfile uses '/etc/loki/local-config.yaml' and CMD '-config.file=/etc/loki/local-config.yaml'. The rock's filename (loki-local-config.yaml) deviates from upstream (local-config.yaml), exactly as the issue states.
fix_hint: Rename 3.7.2/loki-local-config.yaml to 3.7.2/local-config.yaml; in 3.7.2/rockcraft.yaml change the default-config organize mapping to 'local-config.yaml: etc/loki/local-config.yaml' and update the service command to '--config.file=/etc/loki/local-config.yaml'. Decide (per the issue) whether to apply only to the newest dir or retrospectively. Validate via goss /config and 'just test'.
notes: Real maintainer issue (no label, 0 comments, unclaimed). Cross-references tempo-worker-k8s-operator#71 and an agreement to standardize config paths across HA rocks. Open question in the issue: apply to all versions or only new ones; a beginner can do the newest dir and note the choice. The other 4 open issues are auto-generated vulnerability-scan reports.

title: goss: add a /ready readiness check to the Loki smoke test
suggested_labels: Type: Enhancement; good first issue; testing
summary: The shared root goss.yaml checks the loki process and two HTTP endpoints (/config and /loki/api/v1/status/buildinfo) but does not check Loki's standard /ready readiness endpoint. Adding a /ready check (status 200) would make the kgoss smoke test verify the server is actually ready to serve, not just up.
why_good_first_issue: Tiny declarative addition to an existing goss http block, copying the pattern already there. /ready is a well-known Loki endpoint, so no guesswork. Done-state: a /ready http check that passes under 'just test'. No build knowledge needed.
repro_steps: gh api repos/canonical/loki-rock/contents/goss.yaml --jq .content | base64 -d  (only 'process: loki: running' plus http checks for http://localhost:3100/config and .../loki/api/v1/status/buildinfo; no /ready check).
repro_result: confirmed-by-inspection: goss.yaml has process.loki.running plus exactly two http checks (config -> http://localhost:3100/config, buildinfo -> http://localhost:3100/loki/api/v1/status/buildinfo). There is no check for http://localhost:3100/ready, Loki's standard readiness probe, nor for /metrics.
fix_hint: Add under http: a 'ready: {status: 200, url: http://localhost:3100/ready}' entry (note /ready can briefly return 503 right after start, so the maintainer may want goss retries). Optionally add a /metrics check. Validate with 'just test'.
notes: Secondary to issue #81. No version-bump available: 3.7.2/ already pins source-tag v3.7.2, which equals upstream grafana/loki releases/latest (v3.7.2). The goss.yaml is shared across all version dirs, so one edit improves every build.
